changelog:
Mid-work disconnect fix (HTTP/2 keepalive + downstream heartbeat) and web-search decode fix.

Two independent reliability fixes for Claude Code (and any streaming client) running through the proxy.

=== 1) Mid-work disconnects ("context deadline exceeded ... while reading body") ===

Symptom: a long-running turn would die mid-stream after a multi-minute hang, surfacing the Go error 'context deadline exceeded (Client.Timeout or context cancellation while reading body)' as an inline SSE error.

Root cause (confirmed by a 4-dimension audit + adversarial review): the proxy holds a long-lived HTTP/2 keep-alive connection to AWS, but buildKiroTransport set ForceAttemptHTTP2=true WITHOUT configuring the internal http2 transport's ReadIdleTimeout/PingTimeout (both default 0 = disabled). During a quiet stretch (a thinking pause, a slow tool gap) a middlebox — AWS ALB/NLB idle reaper, NAT rebind, outbound proxy, wifi handoff — silently drops the connection with no FIN/RST. Go never probes the dead socket, so resp.Body.Read() blocks until idleTimeoutReader cancels at the 5-minute streamIdleTimeout, producing that error after a long hang. Once message_start was sent, no failover is possible, so it surfaces to the client mid-turn.

Fixes (proxy/kiro.go, proxy/handler.go, main.go):
- HTTP/2 active PINGs on the direct upstream via http2.ConfigureTransports — ReadIdleTimeout=15s, PingTimeout=15s. PINGs keep middleboxes from reaping the idle (thinking) connection AND detect a genuinely dead one in ~30s instead of 5 minutes. Added golang.org/x/net dependency.
- OS-level TCP keepalive dialer (net.Dialer{KeepAlive:15s}) on BOTH transports — this is the only active liveness probe on the proxied/HTTP-1.1 path, where h2 PINGs are unavailable (a forward proxy disables h2). Closes the blind spot the adversarial review flagged.
- Downstream SSE heartbeat: handleClaudeStream now emits an Anthropic `ping` event every 15s while streaming, so a legitimately quiet generation never looks like a dead connection to the client's own idle timer. All Claude-path SSE writes funnel through one mutex-guarded path so the heartbeat can't race the content writer; it's joined before terminal frames so a ping can never follow message_stop.
- Rolling write deadline: the server's fixed WriteTimeout=30min is a guillotine that Go never extends on Flush, so a healthy stream past 30min was cut. The heartbeat now pushes the write deadline forward 2min on each tick via http.NewResponseController — healthy long streams survive, a genuinely dead downstream write still fails.

Residual (documented, not yet changed): the 5-minute streamIdleTimeout is left as-is, so a genuinely healthy-but-silent upstream stream >5min would still be cancelled with the same error. The h2 PINGs keep the connection alive but don't reset the application-level idle reader. Rare in practice; a configurable idle timeout is the follow-up.

=== 2) Web search reported "not available" when it actually worked ===

Symptom: Claude Code's WebSearch tool produced 'web search is not available in the session' even with the feature enabled.

Root cause: NOT actually unavailable. Kiro's /mcp endpoint ran the search and returned results, but `publishedDate` comes back from Kiro as a NUMBER (a year like 2026, or an epoch like 980035200000) while WebSearchResult.publishedDate was typed string. Go's decoder rejected the WHOLE payload ('cannot unmarshal number into Go struct field ...publishedDate of type string'), so every result was discarded. On the parse failure the agentic loop feeds the model 'Web search is currently unavailable...', which the model relays — making a working feature look broken.

Fix (proxy/mcp_websearch.go): custom UnmarshalJSON on WebSearchResult + jsonScalarToString helper that coerces string/number/bool/null scalars to string (integral numbers via strconv.FormatInt so an epoch stays '980035200000', not '9.8e+11'). Verified live: rebuilt the container and re-ran the exact failing web_search request — it now returns proper server_tool_use + web_search_tool_result blocks with real hits.

Validation: go build, go vet, go test ./... all green. New tests: proxy/kiro_http2_test.go (h2 ping wiring + detection-budget-below-idle-window + proxied path skips h2), proxy/heartbeat_test.go (tick/stop lifecycle, idempotent stop, no ticks after stop, interval sanity), and proxy/websearch_test.go additions (numeric/epoch/string publishedDate coercion). Note: go test -race needs cgo (no C compiler here), so the heartbeat concurrency is covered by explicit lifecycle tests instead.

--- 1.0.10-A6 ---

Per-model reasoning-effort analytics on the dashboard.

The Analytics tab now shows, for every model, a breakdown of which reasoning-effort level drove it and the cost of each level — so you can see at a glance how much traffic ran at high/xhigh/max vs. default, and the tokens each level consumed.

What you see: under each model's usage bar, a row of compact chips — one per effort level (low -> max, ordered, with "default" last for requests that carried no graded effort). Each chip shows the request count and tokens for that level, with a tooltip adding the credit cost. Models only ever driven at default show no chips (nothing interesting to surface).

How it's wired (additive, no DB migration):
- Storage reuses the existing daily_stats table with a new scope_type 'model_effort' and scope_id '<canonicalModel>\x1f<effort>'. A row is written on EVERY request alongside the existing per-model row, so the per-effort buckets always sum exactly to the per-model total. Unset/absent effort buckets under 'default'. The 0x1f Unit Separator can't appear in a canonical model id or an effort level, so the two halves are always recoverable.
- The resolved effort level is captured once (Handler.applyReasoningEffort already computes it) and stashed on KiroPayload.ResolvedEffort (json:"-", never serialized to the wire), then read back at record time — so no handler signature had to grow an effort parameter on the hot path.
- New stats APIs: stats.Record now takes an effort arg; stats.ByModelEffort() returns map[model]map[effort]Totals; stats.CanonicalEffort normalizes the bucket label. GET /admin/api/modelstats nests an 'effort' map into each model entry (requests/tokens/credits per level).
- Frontend: renderEffortBreakdown renders the chips; i18n keys analytics.effortLabel / analytics.effortDefault added in both EN and ZH; the byModelHint copy now mentions the effort split.

Failure attribution included: recordFailure / handleUpstreamError / runWithFailover now thread the resolved effort through the terminal-failure path too, so a failed request that asked for effort=high is attributed to the high bucket instead of collapsing into default. The per-effort sum-equals-model-total invariant holds for failures as well (both rows increment together with zero tokens/credits on failure).

Scope notes: requests on the Claude /v1/messages path and the web-search loop carry no graded effort (Anthropic expresses effort via thinking, not a level), so they correctly bucket as 'default'. Historical days recorded before this release have per-model rows but no per-effort rows; the UI degrades gracefully (no chips) rather than showing wrong data.

Validation: go build, go vet, go test ./... all green. New tests in stats/effort_stats_test.go: CanonicalEffort normalization, the full breakdown with merged model spellings + a failed request bucketed by its requested level, the sum-equals-model-total invariant, empty-model skip, and scope-id split. An adversarial review traced effort survival across all 8 success sites + failover and confirmed no divergence.

--- 1.0.10-A5 ---

Dashboard realtime + token-refresh-before-expiry, opt-in global rate limit, and NATIVE reasoning-effort forwarding.

Three independent improvements bundled into one release.

=== 1) Native reasoning effort (the headline) ===

Reasoning effort (low/medium/high/xhigh/max) is now forwarded to Kiro NATIVELY for the models that support it, instead of being collapsed to a thinking on/off flag. This corrects an earlier assumption: the Kiro generateAssistantResponse backend DOES accept a graded effort value.

How it was verified: by capturing the real wire traffic of the installed Kiro CLI 2.5.0 (KIRO_LOG_LEVEL=trace) and reading its own request serializer. Effort is carried as a Bedrock-style passthrough at the TOP LEVEL of the request (sibling of conversationState):

    "additionalModelRequestFields": { "output_config": { "effort": "high" } }

Support is PER MODEL and the backend validates it — sending effort to a model that doesn't declare it returns HTTP 400 ("model does not support additional fields"). Each model advertises its accepted levels via additionalModelRequestFieldsSchema in ListAvailableModels. Observed enums (from a live account):
- Opus 4.6 / 4.7 / 4.8 / auto:  low, medium, high, xhigh, max
- Sonnet 4 / 4.5 / 4.6, Opus 4.5:  low, medium, high, max  (no xhigh)
- Haiku 4.5, DeepSeek, MiniMax, GLM, Qwen:  no effort support

Implementation (safe against the 400 by construction — we never send a level a model didn't advertise):
- KiroPayload gained AdditionalModelRequestFields (top-level, omitempty).
- ModelInfo now captures additionalModelRequestFieldsSchema; the model cache merge preserves it.
- reasoning_effort.go: xhigh is now a DISTINCT level (was wrongly folded into max); modelEffortLevels parses the per-model enum; resolveModelEffort clamps a requested level DOWN to the nearest supported one (never escalates a user to a higher-credit tier); buildEffortRequestFields shapes the output_config.effort payload.
- Handler.applyReasoningEffort resolves the model's cached schema and populates the field only when supported, merging without clobbering other passthrough keys.
- Wired into OpenAI Chat Completions (reasoning_effort), OpenAI Responses / Codex (reasoning.effort) HTTP + WebSocket. Claude /v1/messages stays on the thinking path (Anthropic has no graded effort field).
- Fallback preserved: models WITHOUT native effort support keep the prior behavior — effort maps to whether adaptive thinking is engaged (minimal -> off, else on) — so nothing regresses.
- Corrected stale comments across handler.go / translator.go / tests that claimed "Kiro rejects reasoning_effort" (true on older builds, no longer true on 2.5).

Tests: reasoning_effort_test.go (normalization incl. xhigh, engage mapping, schema parsing, clamp-down gating) and reasoning_effort_apply_test.go (end-to-end against a seeded model cache: opus high->high, opus xhigh->xhigh, sonnet xhigh->high clamp, sonnet max->max, haiku/unknown/minimal/unset -> field omitted, other passthrough keys preserved).

=== 2) Dashboard realtime + refresh-before-expiry ===

Account list and per-account counters on the dashboard now update in realtime over the existing status WebSocket, and tokens are refreshed before they can lapse.
- dashboardSnapshot now carries a per-account accountStats array (enabled, ban status, expiresAt, hasToken, usage current/limit/percent, trial fields, request/error counts, total tokens, total credits, last used, last refresh). The browser merges these live into the account cards (mergeAccountStats) and re-renders without a manual refresh — credit usage shows as it's consumed. The merge skips while a detail/add modal is open so it can't clobber an in-progress edit.
- Broadcast ordering fixed: pool.UpdateStats now runs BEFORE recordSuccess at all success sites, so the snapshot the broadcast captures already includes the just-finished request's tokens/credits (previously the dashboard lagged one request behind).
- Token refresh before expiry: the 5-minute background sweep now uses a wider 11-minute skew (backgroundTokenRefreshSkewSeconds) when deciding whether to refresh an account's token, so an idle token can't lapse in the gap between ticks. The per-request refresh skew (120s) is unchanged.

=== 3) Opt-in global rate limit ===

New optional global token-bucket limiter across all inbound API traffic (proxy/global_ratelimit.go), DISABLED by default (globalRateLimitPerMinute=0). When set, it caps requests/minute with a smooth refill and returns 429 + Retry-After in the right shape per format (Claude / OpenAI / Responses). Configurable live via admin Settings (globalRateLimitPerMinute) — takes effect without restart. When zero it is a no-op on the hot path.

Validation: go build, go vet, go test ./... all green. New tests: reasoning_effort_test.go, reasoning_effort_apply_test.go, global_ratelimit_test.go (token-bucket allow/deny/refill/disabled), dashboard_snapshot_test.go (per-account stats in snapshot).

--- 1.0.10-A4 ---

API key restriction fixes: model whitelist, token/credit caps, and expiry now enforce correctly.

Three enforcement bugs fixed so a key gets exactly what it's allowed.

1) /v1/models leaked the full catalog regardless of the key's model whitelist. It was the only /v1/* route that skipped validateApiKey, so the matched key never landed in the request context and the per-key model filter in handleModels was silently skipped. Fix: /v1/models now validates the API key like the other routes — it both enforces auth on the endpoint (previously none) and populates the context the filter needs. A restricted key now lists only its allowed models.

2) Restrictions were ignored when API-key auth was optional. validateApiKey returned early without matching the presented key when RequireApiKey was off, so a recognized restricted key's whitelist/quotas never applied. Fix: a recognized key is always matched and stashed; 'auth optional' now only means an absent/unknown key is allowed through — a presented key's restrictions always take effect.

3) Periodic token & credit limits never tripped (the big one). ConsumeAPIKey (post-response accounting) returned WITHOUT recording usage whenever a request would cross the daily token/credit cap. The counter froze just below the limit, so the pre-flight gate's `DailyTokens >= limit` check never fired and the key served forever. Fix: ConsumeAPIKey now ALWAYS records actual usage (the response already streamed — it can't gate the current request), then auto-disables only on hard caps (absolute/lazy expiry, lifetime request/token/credit). The crossing is persisted so the NEXT pre-flight rejects; periodic caps reset organically on the period boundary and never disable the key. Request-count and lifetime caps were unaffected by the original bug.

Verified working across all four request paths (Claude messages, OpenAI chat, Responses SSE, Responses WebSocket) and the web-search branch: request-time model enforcement (with dotted/dashed alias resolution), absolute + lazy expiry, lifetime caps, and minute/hour rate limits all gate before burning upstream quota.

Tests: proxy/models_route_filter_test.go (3 end-to-end tests through ServeHTTP with a real key header — the path the model bug lived in: filters to allowed models, rejects bad keys, restricts even when auth is optional); config/apikeys_limits_test.go (7 tests: periodic token/credit crossing recorded then next request rejected, period rollover re-enables, lifetime cap disables, absolute + lazy expiry disable, disabled-key still records usage). go build, go vet, go test ./... all green.

--- 1.0.10-A3 ---

Native web search (default ON) + verified tool-call / image pass-through.

Web search now works in Claude Code (and any client that sends Anthropic's hosted web_search tool). Previously the hosted web_search tool was silently dropped because Kiro/CodeWhisperer has no server-side tools. Now we emulate it the same way the popular Kiro proxies do (jwadow/kiro-gateway ~1.8k stars, aliom-v/KiroGate): by calling Kiro's OWN native MCP web_search endpoint (https://q.<region>.amazonaws.com/mcp) with the account's existing token. No external search provider, no API key, no signup — the search runs on Amazon's side and bills against the same Kiro account.

Enabled by DEFAULT (matching those projects' verified behavior). config.WebSearchEnabled is a *bool: nil means default-on; set it explicitly to false to opt out, via the admin Settings (webSearchEnabled) toggle.

How it works (agentic loop, proxy/websearch_loop.go):
- web_search is exposed to the upstream model as a callable function tool instead of being dropped.
- When the model calls web_search, the proxy executes the search via the native MCP endpoint, feeds the results back as a tool_result, and re-runs the model — looping up to 4 rounds until it produces an answer.
- The final response carries native server_tool_use + web_search_tool_result blocks so Claude Code renders real citations. Works for both streaming (replayed as SSE) and non-streaming responses.

Safety / isolation:
- The loop runs ONLY when web search is enabled AND the request actually carries a web_search tool. Every other request takes the original, unchanged handler + streaming + failover path, so the stable baseline behavior is untouched.
- Graceful degradation: if the /mcp endpoint is unavailable on an account tier/region or the call fails, the loop feeds the model an 'unavailable' note and lets it answer from training — a request never breaks because web search is missing. Main generation rounds still go through full pool failover.
- New diagnostic endpoint POST /admin/api/websearch/probe runs one real /mcp search against an account and returns the raw outcome, so operators can confirm availability and see the exact response shape.

Tool calls + images (verified, unchanged): custom tools and MCP tools round-trip correctly through the existing toolUse/toolResult pairing; image input (base64, data URLs, multiple media_type field variants, OpenAI-style image parts) is handled. Other Anthropic hosted tools (computer use, hosted code execution) remain unsupported by design — Kiro can't execute them.

New files: proxy/mcp_websearch.go (MCP client + response parser), proxy/websearch_blocks.go (detection, tool spec, native block shaping), proxy/websearch_loop.go (agentic loop + buffered SSE emitter), proxy/websearch_test.go (14 unit tests). Updated convertClaudeTools to forward web_search when enabled; added the admin probe + settings toggle. Tests: web-search detection/parse/shaping fully unit-tested; translator server-tool tests updated for the new default-on behavior plus a disabled-path test. go build, go vet, go test ./... all green. Note: the pure logic is unit-tested; the live /mcp round-trip shape is confirmed via the probe endpoint against a real account.

--- 1.0.10-A2 ---

Token usage accuracy fix: stop discarding the real upstream token counts.

What was wrong:
- All four response finalization paths (Claude stream/non-stream, OpenAI stream/non-stream) captured the exact input/output token counts from the Kiro event stream in OnComplete, then unconditionally overwrote outputTokens with a local char-heuristic estimate — throwing away the real number on every single response. Input-token precedence was also inverted: the coarse contextUsagePercentage-derived value (rounded to a percentage) was preferred over the exact upstream count.
- This directly corrupted the usage object we return. Open-source CLIs (Claude Code, opencode, Cline, Codex) read our reported usage verbatim for their context-window bars, cost estimates, and auto-compaction timing — none of them recount client-side or call count_tokens for context tracking — so wrong usage numbers meant wrong context %, wrong cost, and mistimed compaction in every client.

Fix:
- Output tokens: the local estimate now runs only as a fallback (if outputTokens <= 0), so the exact upstream count survives whenever Kiro provides it.
- Input tokens: extracted the precedence into a single pure helper resolveInputTokens(upstream, contextDerived, estimated) applied at all four sites. Order, most to least accurate: exact upstream count -> contextUsagePercentage x context window -> local char estimate. An exact upstream count is never overwritten by a coarser fallback.
- Behavior is never worse than before: when upstream sends no counts we still fall back to the estimate; when it does, we now report the truth.

Tests: added proxy/token_precedence_test.go locking the precedence (upstream wins even when smaller, context-derived used only without upstream, estimate only as last resort, negative-as-absent). go build, go vet, and go test ./... all green.

--- 1.0.10-A1 ---

Login fix + automated model discovery, grouping removal, one-click toggles, and per-request account failover.

Critical fix - admin login:
- The admin dashboard login was completely broken: a JS syntax error in the API-key model-checkbox-grid builder (escaped single quotes inside an already single-quoted string literal) failed to parse the entire inline <script> block, so login() and every other dashboard function were undefined and the login button did nothing. Replaced the invalid escapes; the whole script block now parses and the dashboard works again.

Automated model fetching (no more hardcoded list):
- The model catalog is now persisted as last-known-good (config.KnownModels). On a successful upstream ListAvailableModels fetch the catalog is saved; on restart the in-memory cache is seeded from it so /v1/models serves the real ids immediately instead of a hardcoded guess. fallbackAnthropicModels now derives from the persisted catalog and only uses a minimal bootstrap list on a truly fresh install that has never reached upstream.
- Claude Code dashed ids (claude-opus-4-7) are always emitted alongside the dotted upstream alias (claude-opus-4.7) via the existing mechanical dot->dash derivation, so new minors keep working without a code change.

Removed grouping:
- Deleted the per-API-key Group field and per-account Groups across config, pool, handlers, admin API, and the web UI (the Account Group input on API keys + the Groups editor on the account detail modal). Routing now depends on the model allowlist only. Existing configs that still carry group/groups JSON keys are silently ignored (graceful back-compat). Removed pool/group_test.go; added coverage elsewhere.

One-click toggles:
- Each account card now has a one-click Overage toggle (in addition to the existing one-click Enable/Disable), with an optimistic instant repaint and a matching .btn-warning style. The global Allow-Over-Usage switch in Settings is unchanged.

Per-request account failover + performance:
- New runWithFailover dispatcher: when an upstream call fails BEFORE any byte reaches the client (429/quota, 5xx, or connection error), the request transparently rotates to a different eligible account instead of returning a 5xx while healthy peers exist. Streaming handlers (Claude + OpenAI chat) defer the first SSE frame until the first upstream byte so failover is safe; once streaming starts the response is committed and a later error is surfaced inline. Auth (401/403), payment (402/OVERAGE), and client-cancellation are terminal and never burn extra accounts. Capped at 3 attempts. The Responses (Codex) path keeps single-account behavior.
- Failure accounting is per-request: a request that rotates across N accounts cools each distinct account but counts as exactly ONE failed request (matching pre-failover dashboard semantics); a failover-then-succeed counts as zero failures.
- Pool selection no longer nests the config lock under the pool lock (strategy resolved before locking) — removes a hot-path lock-ordering hazard.
- Raised the Kiro transport MaxIdleConnsPerHost 20 -> 100 (and MaxIdleConns -> 200): nearly all traffic targets one host, so the per-host idle pool now keeps warm keep-alive connections for the whole concurrent working set instead of churning TLS handshakes.
- The per-request [Stats] INFO log is now gated on log level so the success hot path skips the getCredits() lock + arg formatting above INFO.

Tests: added failover classifier + dispatcher tests (retryable vs terminal, single-failure accounting, rotate-then-succeed), pool exclude-set tests, and config KnownModels round-trip tests. All packages green on go test ./....
